title: Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics
description: Science fiction author Isaac Asimov introduces the Three Laws of Robotics in his short story 'Runaround', forming the earliest theoretical foundation for AI safety and regulation.

title: First UN CCW Meeting on LAWS
description: The United Nations Convention on Certain Conventional Weapons (CCW) holds its first informal meeting of experts to discuss questions related to Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems (LAWS).

title: Asilomar AI Principles
description: Created by the Future of Life Institute, a set of 23 principles designed to ensure that AI remains beneficial to humanity, endorsed by thousands of AI researchers.

title: GDPR Goes Into Effect
description: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) becomes enforceable in the EU. Article 22 specifically regulates automated decision-making and profiling, granting users the 'right to an explanation'.

title: OECD AI Principles
description: The OECD adopts its Principles on Artificial Intelligence, the first intergovernmental standard on AI, promoting innovative and trustworthy AI that respects human rights and democratic values.

title: EU AI Act Proposed
description: The European Commission proposes the Artificial Intelligence Act, introducing a risk-based approach to AI regulation, banning 'unacceptable risk' applications like social scoring.

title: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights
description: The US White House Office of Science and Technology Policy releases a non-binding blueprint outlining five core principles to guide the design, use, and deployment of automated systems.

title: Bletchley Declaration
description: At the first global AI Safety Summit held at Bletchley Park, 28 countries including the US, UK, and China sign a declaration acknowledging the risks of frontier AI and committing to global cooperation.

title: EU AI Act Passed
description: The European Parliament officially passes the AI Act, creating the world's first comprehensive legal framework for artificial intelligence, setting a global standard for AI regulation.

title: First UN General Assembly AI Resolution
description: The UN General Assembly unanimously adopts a resolution promoting safe, secure, and trustworthy artificial intelligence systems, emphasizing human rights and sustainable development.
